items.title: Trump doubles down in criticising Pope Leo over Iran
items.summary: The pope termed Mr. Trump’s threat this month to destroy ​the Iranian civilisation as unacceptable and previously declined to join the U.S. President’s so-called “Board of Peace” ⁠initiative for Gaza

items.title: Children killed in Lebanon as Israeli strikes hit homes far from front lines of war with Hezbollah
items.summary: “Israeli military didn't deny that children have been killed in its Lebanon strikes but said it has targeted Hezbollah facilities and militants,” media report

items.title: Xi Jinping calls China-Russia ties 'precious' in current international context
items.summary: During a meeting with Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov in Beijing, Mr. Xi said the strong vitality and exemplary significance of the friendship treaty between the two countries stand out even more under such a backdrop

items.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Trump not considering extending ceasefire with Iran, says 'deal is preferable'
items.summary: Trump expressed confidence about imminent developments, saying, "I think you're going to be watching an amazing two days ahead. I really do."

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar promises to suspend state media broadcasts, ensure press freedom
items.summary: Critics say public media served as a government mouthpiece under former PM Viktor Orban and accused him of presiding over the undermining of independent media as allies of ‌his Fidesz party took control of private ​outlets — charges he has denied

items.title: Immigrants should think of themselves as Americans first: Vance
items.summary: The U.S. V-P said he is married to the daughter of Indian immigrants, who have greatly contributed to the U.S., but his father-in-law never asked him to do anything specifically in the interest of his country of origin

items.title: Attorney for suspect in attack at Sam Altman’s home says he was in midst of ’mental health crisis’
items.summary: The public defender for a man accused of throwing an incendiary device at Sam Altman’s home says her client has autism and was experiencing an “acute mental health crisis.”

items.title: Jaishankar speaks to Israeli counterpart Gideon Sa'ar over ‘different aspects’ of West Asia crisis
items.summary: The phone conversation between the two foreign ministers came amid reports of efforts by the U.S. and Iran to hold a fresh round of negotiations after the collapse of the initial dialogue in Islamabad

items.title: China calls U.S. claims of military pressure on Taiwan a 'distortion'
items.summary: China has stepped up ⁠military activity around democratically governed Taiwan, which it views as its own territory

items.title: North Korea is boosting nuclear weapons capacity: IAEA chief
items.summary: North Korea's nuclear programme was estimated at a ⁠few dozen warheads, Grossi told a news conference, citing signs of activity such as the operation of a light water reactor and activation of other facilities besides Yongbyon

items.title: Over 80% nations got loans from China in the last two decades
items.summary: Chinese banks have lent about $200 billion to U.S. projects and initiatives, which makes the U.S. the biggest beneficiary

items.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 stranded Iranian sailors: Minister
items.summary: Deputy Defence Minister Aruna Jayasekara said 32 sailors rescued from the IRIS Dena — a frigate attacked on March 4 just off Sri Lanka — and another 206 from the IRIS Bushehr left on April 14, 2026

items.title: Sanctioned tanker turns back to Strait of Hormuz, day after Gulf exit
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump announced the blockade on Sunday (April 12, 2026) after weekend ‌peace talks in Islamabad between the U.S. and Iran failed to reach a deal

items.summary: Overcrowding, strong winds and rough seas caused the vessel to lose control and sink

items.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to direct negotiations after 'productive' talks: U.S.
items.summary: The announcement came after Israeli and Lebanese envoys held more than two hours of talks mediated by U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio

items.title: Another U.S. strike on suspected drug boat in the eastern Pacific kills four
items.summary: The latest strike brings the death toll to 175 since the operations began in early September

items.title: IMF cuts growth outlook, warns world already drifting toward more adverse scenario
items.summary: Under the ⁠IMF’s worst-case outlook, the global economy teeters on the brink of recession, with oil prices averaging $110 a barrel in 2026 and $125 in 2027

items.summary: New Delhi has underscored that it rightly deserves a permanent seat at the horse-shoe table

items.title: Carney suspends Canada's fuel tax in first act after securing majority government
items.summary: Carney said he wants to focus on affordability, housing and accelerating major economic projects

items.title: Israel-Iran war highlights: Vice President Vance says U.S.-Iran mistrust cannot be solved overnight
items.summary: The IMF says that Iran, Iraq, Qatar among hardest-hit economies; Pakistan proposes new U.S.-Iran talks as Vance and Trump hint at progress

items.title: Republicans are moving to fund Homeland Security ’the hard way’ after end of talks
items.summary: Democrats say they will continue to insist on reforms to the agencies.

items.title: Lebanese and Israeli envoys to meet as Israel presses war on Hezbollah
items.summary: The meeting comes at a critical juncture in the crisis in West Asia, a week into a fragile ceasefire between the United States, Israel, and Iran. The parallel war between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon has been a complicating factor in Pakistan’s mediation to end the wider conflict

items.title: China terms U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz 'dangerous and irresponsible' move
items.summary: Beijing also denied aiding Iran militarily and threatened to hit back if U.S. President Donald Trump hikes tariffs against China on the allegation of helping Tehran

items.title: Xi calls for closer ties with Spain in face of global 'chaos'
items.summary: "Both China and Spain are principled countries that stand for justice. They should strengthen communication, consolidate mutual trust, and cooperate closely to oppose the world's regression to the law of the jungle," Mr. Xi said during talks in the Great Hall of the People

items.title: Russian missile kills five in Ukraine's Dnipro, officials say
items.summary: “These were civilians who ‌were simply driving ‌down ⁠the road in their ⁠cars. People who were going about their business, heading home ​to their ‌families,” Ukraine’s Infrastructure Minister Oleksiy Kuleba said on the Telegram app

items.title: Watch: The Hindu’s new show - Above the Fold | 14.04.2026
items.summary: Above the Fold is The Hindu’s weekday news show, bringing you the most important stories of the day with clarity, depth, and context. Each episode also spotlights exclusive reporting, sharp analysis, and on-ground stories from The Hindu that you shouldn’t miss.

items.title: Pope Leo issues warning on democracy after Trump criticism
items.summary: “Lacking this foundation, [democracy] risks becoming ‌either a ⁠majoritarian tyranny or a mask ⁠for the dominance of economic and technological elites,” says Pope in the letter

items.title: Israel-Iran war highlights: Trump vows to destroy Iranian warships that get near U.S. blockade

items.title: Nearly 700 reported killed in Sudan drone strikes this year: UN
items.summary: "In the first three months of this year, nearly 700 civilians were reportedly killed in drone strikes," UN humanitarian chief Tom Fletcher said in a statement, a day before the three-year anniversary of the start of the war

items.title: School shooting in Turkiye leaves 16 wounded
items.summary: The assailant, a 19-year-old former student, later took his own life as ⁠police attempted to apprehend him

items.summary: A Defence Ministry source said one of ‌the consequences is that Italy will no longer cooperate with Israel on military training

items.title: Foreign secretary Misri reaches Berlin for India-Germany Foreign Office consultations
items.summary: Discussions expected to cover diverse domains of bilateral cooperation, including trade and investment, defence and security, technology, green energy, development cooperation, education and people-to-people ties as well as global and regional matters of mutual interest.

items.title: U.S., Iran may meet again in Islamabad for second round of talks by next week: Report
items.summary: The contacts are ongoing for the revival of stalled negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, and talks may be held again before the end of the two-week ceasefire on April 21, says reports citing highly placed sources

items.summary: Police in San Francisco, a hub for tech development, said they had responded after reports that someone had tried to set fire to a gate at the sprawling home

items.title: 'Stop hiring humans'? Silicon Valley confronts AI job panic
items.summary: The anxiety is well-founded. More and more companies are directly citing AI in announcing job cuts

items.title: China says policy to improve relations with India remains unchanged
items.summary: China has been publishing its names for various regions in Arunachal Pradesh since 2017 for which India has been consistently objecting, saying that “fictitious names" to Indian territory cannot alter "undeniable reality"

items.title: West Asia conflict threatens to push 2.5 million people in India into poverty: UNDP report
items.summary: “In India, poverty is expected to rise from around 4,00,000 to 2.5 million,” the report said

items.summary: Voters cast ballots for three vacant seats of the 343 districts represented in Parliament

items.title: Donald Trump vows U.S. will sink any Iran boats that challenge blockade
items.summary: The U.S. military said the blockade would begin at 1930 hours IST and apply to all ships leaving or seeking to dock at Iranian harbours, but as the deadline passed there was no announcement that the measure had come into effect, nor sign of any interceptions

items.title: Hezbollah chief urges Lebanese government to cancel Washington talks with Israel
items.summary: Hundreds of Hezbollah supporters protested on Friday (April 10, 2026) and Saturday (April 11, 2026) against the planned talks, accusing Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam of being a "zionist"

items.title: U.S. lawmaker Swalwell resigns over sexual misconduct allegations
items.summary: Eric Swalwell's troubles escalated rapidly over the weekend as reports in the San Francisco Chronicle and CNN detailed allegations from four women

items.title: Trump nominates former lawmaker Michelle Steel as U.S. ambassador to South Korea
items.summary: A senior State Department ​official, Kevin Kim, ‌has been serving as acting ambassador since last October

items.title: IMF, World Bank, IEA urge countries to stop hoarding energy supplies, imposing export controls
items.summary: IEA Executive Director Birol said earlier that the conflict had triggered the worst global energy disruption ever, with more than 80 ‌oil and gas facilities across West Asia damaged to date.

items.title: Israeli fire kills three in Gaza following new ceasefire talks
items.summary: Medics said the strike had hit a group of men outside a school in Deir al-Balah in the central Gaza Strip. There was no immediate comment by the Israeli military

items.title: Israel says troops fighting Hezbollah surrounded key Lebanon town
items.summary: "The forces of the 98th Division have completed the encirclement of the town of Bint Jbeil and have begun an assault on it," the military's Arabic-language spokesman Colonel Avichay Adraee said on X.

items.title: Russia offers to take in Iran's enriched uranium
items.summary: Failed talks between Iran and the United States over the weekend dashed hopes of a swift deal to permanently end the war that has killed thousands and thrown the global economy into turmoil since it began in late February

items.title: Iran executed at least 1,639 in 2025, more hangings feared: NGOs
items.summary: The number of executions represented an increase of 6% on the 975 people Iran put to death in 2024, and also included 48 women, Norway-based Iran Human Rights (IHR) and Paris-based Together Against the Death Penalty (ECPM) said in their joint annual report

items.title: U.S., Israel met ‘strategic defeat’ in Iran, says Ambassador Fathali
items.summary: The Envoy said the U.S. side made "unlawful" demands during the talks that led to the negotiations ending without results

items.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against WSJ, Murdoch over reporting on ties to Epstein
items.summary: The ruling marks yet another blow in the Trump administration’s efforts to manage fallout over its release of the Epstein files and the President’s attempts to use the legal system to chill reporting he finds critical of him

items.title: India, Oman discuss ways to boost trade, investments
items.summary: India will reduce tariffs on Omani products such as dates, marbles and petrochemical items, under the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement

items.title: Pope says he will continue to speak out against war after Trump attack
items.summary: “I don’t ‌want to get into a debate with him,” Pope Leo said; “I don’t think that ‌the message of the ​Gospel is meant to be abused in the way ⁠that some people are doing,” he said
